---
title: "Knowledge bundles"
description: "A wiki authored to be consumed by other people's LLMs: portable, shareable compiled competence, with an OKF import/export path for interoperability."
date: "2026-06-11"
categories: [bundles]
---

A **knowledge bundle** is a wiki authored with the intent that other people consume it through their own LLMs. Bundles are wikis, but not every wiki is a bundle; the distinguishing property is intent to share and be queried by non-authors.

The point of a bundle is that it ships **compiled competence**, not just searchable content. A student learning knowledge engineering opens an ask session against a bundle on that domain and gets the group's accumulated judgment, not a reading list. Bundles come in two shapes: a *reference* bundle uses a neutral retrieve-synthesize-cite protocol; a *pedagogical* bundle ships a Socratic or worked-example protocol embedded in its own content, so the teaching travels with the material.

Because a bundle is just a git repo of markdown plus a reified graph, it is portable: you can take it to another tool. The roadmap adds OKF-compatible import and export so bundles interoperate with other open-knowledge tooling rather than living in a walled garden. That portability is the whole point, your memory is yours, and it moves.

This is the shareable unit of the ecosystem: the thing a group publishes and a peer consumes.
